description:
Goal
Make the backend interpreter obey the same reusable-cons model and runtime telemetry contract as compiler2 JIT/native codegen.

Why
The post-.13.6 cleanup exposed two real gaps:
- test/fixture readers still treated reusable_cons_* process_exited measurements as optional even though process_exited is now the authoritative boundary event;
- the backend interpreter rebuilt lists blindly and retained whole continuation environments, so it neither observed reusable-cons attempts/reuse nor published retained refs at the same boundaries as the JIT path.

Scope
- make process_exited reusable_cons_attempts and reusable_cons_reused mandatory in test/fixture readers;
- make the runtime/heap identity contract explicit: reuse_or_alloc_list_cons_raw_kind returns the original ref iff it reused in place;
- teach the backend interpreter to seed reusable-cons sources from entry reusable_cons_captures, extend that map through SplitList, and route single-cell list rebuilds through fz_list_reuse_or_cons_parts;
- shrink backend continuation capture environments to the target entry's declared captures and publish captured refs with fz_mark_published_ref_aliased at continuation materialization boundaries;
- add parity coverage proving compiler2 JIT and backend interpreter agree on reusable-cons exit counters for a mixed reuse/fallback fixture.

Validation
- cargo test -p fz-runtime list_reuse_or_cons_parts_updates_process_counters
- cargo test runtime process_exited parity cases
- cargo test compiler2 reusable-cons runtime/parity cases
- fixture_matrix reusable-cons telemetry contract tests
- cargo check
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings

Acceptance
The backend interpreter should stop allocating/copying blindly at continuation boundaries, reusable-cons exit telemetry should be strict and engine-uniform, and the code/docs should expose one explicit boundary model rather than optional readers or hidden identity assumptions.
title: Backend interpreter: honor reusable-cons captures and strict exit telemetry
